title: Computational Efficiency
concept: The computational complexity of self-attention scales quadratically with resolution, making ViT slower for high-resolution images but highly parallelizable on hardware.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main pre-training requirement for Vision Transformers to beat CNNs?

options.text: They must be pre-trained on massive datasets (millions of images).
They must use a frozen linear projection weight matrix.
They must be trained without any position embeddings.

exercises.explain: Correct! Because of weak inductive bias, ViTs require massive pre-training data to learn spatial relationships.

cards.title: The GPU race
cards.body: Why does a transformer run so fast on a modern GPU compared to a CNN of similar size? It comes down to how operations are laid out. While CNNs do a lot of small sequential steps, ViTs do massive, parallel matrix multiplications that GPUs love.

cards.title: Hardware Efficiency
cards.body: Computational efficiency in ViTs is highly hardware-friendly. Because self-attention relies on large matrix multiplications, it can be heavily parallelized. However, because its complexity is O(N²), memory demands grow quadratically with image resolution, unlike CNN's linear O(N) scaling.

cards.title: Chaining tasks vs. a group project
cards.body: A CNN is like building a house brick-by-brick (each layer relies on the previous local result; very sequential). A ViT is like a massive group project: all members (patches) work in parallel at their desks, and then send their work to a central compiler. It's faster if you have enough desks (GPU cores)!

cards.title: FLOP comparison
cards.body: For a standard 224x224 input, a ViT-Base model requires roughly 18 billion floating-point operations (FLOPs) to run a single image, similar to a ResNet-152, but it often processes faster on hardware because the calculations are less sequential.

cards.title: Not always memory efficient
cards.body: You might think ViTs are always faster than CNNs. However, if you input a high-resolution 1024x1024 image, a CNN's compute cost increases 20 times, while a standard ViT's cost explodes by **400 times** due to quadratic attention!

cards.title: CNN vs. ViT Complexity Scaling
cards.body: How image resolution scales computing cost:

left.text:
Complexity: O(Resolution)
Double resolution -> 4x compute
Highly efficient for large images

right.text:
Complexity: O(Resolution²)
Double resolution -> 16x compute
Explodes for large images

exercises.prompt: Why do ViT models run efficiently on modern GPUs despite their size?
exercises.explain: GPUs are specifically designed for massive parallel matrix multiplications, which make up the bulk of self-attention calculations.

options.text: Their operations consist of large, parallelisable matrix multiplications that hardware is optimized for
They automatically bypass the GPU and run on the CPU
They do not perform any mathematical calculations
They shrink the image to 1x1 before processing

exercises.prompt: True or false: As image resolution increases, a CNN's compute cost grows slower than a standard ViT's compute cost.
exercises.explain: True. CNNs scale linearly with resolution, whereas standard self-attention in ViTs scales quadratically.
Attention compares every patch to every other patch, leading to quadratic (O(N²)) complexity.
exercises.sentence: While CNN computation scales linearly, standard ViT self-attention scales ___ with the number of image patches.

exercises.prompt: Which of these tasks is standard ViT least suited for due to its quadratic scaling?
exercises.explain: Standard ViT's quadratic complexity makes high-resolution images extremely expensive, causing memory overload.

options.text: Processing high-resolution 4K images for pixel-perfect segmentation
Detecting objects in standard 224x224 video frames

exercises.prompt: If standard ViT struggles with high resolutions, how do we deploy them for high-resolution tasks in practice?

options.text: By using local window attention variants (like Swin) or downsampling the inputs with early convolutions.
By deleting the self-attention layers entirely.
By training the model on a CPU instead of a GPU.
By forcing the patches to overlap by 99%.

exercises.explain: Incredible! Researchers use clever modifications (like Swin Transformer's local windows) to bring the complexity down to linear scaling. Next: receptive fields comparison!
